slides.kicker: // sample template · water utility and infrastructure
slides.lead: Demo presentation of the template. All text is sample (dummy) content and only exists to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. The event name, a one-line subtitle and a meta row at the bottom belong here. Highlight one word with em for the accent color.
Team slide. Instead of photos it uses initials in a colored circle, so no images are needed.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions between them.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Good at the start of a block.

slides.lead: Network state, data and the biggest bottlenecks.
slides.notes: Divider slide between blocks. Large heading on a colored gradient.

slides.h1: The data is already in the pipes.
slides.lead: Flow meters, pressure sensors and SCADA collect more than anyone can read. Value appears only when it is connected.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: large heading, bold illustration, short caption. Good for a point or a chapter.

items: less non-revenue water lost after leak detection rollout
saved daily on manual meter reporting
higher network pressure stability across the grid
bullets: Measure impact, not activity: the effect on supply is what counts.
Start where the data already exists and where the pain is sharpest.
A small pilot with a clear metric beats a big plan without one.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a large number and a caption, supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: The model does not wait for a burst, it predicts it.
bullets: Pressure, flow and acoustic signals from key mains.
Before water surfaces on the street.
Repairs into the window with least disruption.
Field crews validate each flagged zone.
slides.notes: Two column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales itself.

slides.lead: Same pipe, two approaches and two cost curves.
cards: We dig only when a main bursts. Costly and unpredictable.
Service by calendar. Better, but often far too early.
Service by actual condition. The lowest cost.
Critical mains predictive, the rest scheduled.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparison or a list of variants.

slides.lead: Six typical building blocks of a smart water network.
cards: Data from the network in real time, near the source.
Live views of grid pressure and anomalies.
Flags unusual flow earlier than a human could.
Data straight into a daily brief for leadership.
Telemetry stays inside your network, under control.
A team that understands the tools and trusts them.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon in the header of each card. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: Gains slow down, it pays to finish the basics.
Tools improve fast and reshape established routines.
Automation takes over design, not just calculation.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear output.
slides.notes: A horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a heading and a small caption.

slides.quote: It is not the utility with the most sensors that wins, but the one that turns a reading into a decision fastest.
slides.by: Sample quote to showcase the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

bullets: Every tool has the name of a person who answers for it.
You know in advance how you will recognize success.
On critical steps a person always decides.
slides.notes: A plain bullet slide with a strong lead word. Text scales up automatically to fill the area.

slides.lead: A pilot without a metric and an owner ends up in a drawer.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one large sentence centered on the page. Use it for a key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this quarter.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.h1: A report from data in 30 seconds.
slides.lead: We switch into the tool and build a daily network brief live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or a break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. Centered heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and a call to action.
